obligations.reason: Debug interfaces (dmi_jtag/SimDTM) may reach peripheral registers without honoring the register lock (reglk_ctrl/we_flag). Verify that debug-originated reads/writes to locked peripherals are blocked when the lock is active.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug interface (i_dmi_jtag, i_SimDTM) acting through dm_slave bus
obligations.object: peripheral registers protected by reglk_ctrl and we_flag[0..4]
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock and/or reglk write-enable flags must be valid before debug transaction reaches the locked device
obligations.candidate_path: tck_i/tms_i/td_i → dmi_jtag → dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata → internal bus routing to peripherals
required_evidence_slots: Source code showing debug path to locked peripheral without lock override
RTL signals proving write-enable generation ignores we_flag
Simulation trace or formal proof counterexample
obligations.uncertainty: dmi_jtag internal details are outside the provided fact layer; verify that debug transactions do not have a separate bypass path for we_flag.

obligations.reason: Fuse values may be read without proper authentication or privilege. Check whether fuse_req can be asserted by unauthorized agents (e.g., unprivileged software, debug, test).
obligations.subject: any bus master or internal logic that sets fuse_req
obligations.operation: read fuse data (fuse_rdata) by asserting fuse_req
obligations.expected_guard: fuse_req should be gated by jtag_unlock, or a privilege/state machine that prevents unauthenticated read
obligations.candidate_path: fuse_req generation logic → fuse_addr → fuse_rdata returned to bus or debug
state_conditions_to_check: any lock/authentication state for fuse access
required_evidence_slots: RTL source of fuse_req signal
Combinational or sequential guard conditions
Path from debug interface to fuse_req if any
obligations.uncertainty: Fuse controller is not described in the fact layer; need to examine actual instance as referenced in riscv_peripherals.sv.

obligations.reason: Boot ROM read is gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] but debug_mode_i or testmode_i may bypass this gating or alter priv_lvl_i, allowing unauthorized read of bootrom content.
obligations.subject: CPU in debug mode or hardware override of priv_lvl_i
obligations.expected_guard: rom_req must be deasserted when privilege level is insufficient, even while debug_mode_i is active; testmode_i must not force access
obligations.candidate_path: rom_req_acct → AND with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] → rom_req; debug/test may affect rom_req_acct or priv_lvl_i
required_evidence_slots: Source of rom_req_acct and its dependency on debug/test
Logic that sets priv_lvl_i in debug mode
Waveform showing rom_req asserted when access should be denied
obligations.uncertainty: Need to confirm that rom_req_acct cannot be independently toggled by debug path.

obligations.reason: Write-enable flags (we_flag_0..4) are critical for locking peripherals. Check whether debug or test paths can directly assert these flags without unlocking the register lock.
obligations.subject: debug interface (dm_slave) or test mode controller
obligations.expected_guard: we_flag should be driven by lock state machine; debug must not bypass the lock condition
obligations.candidate_path: from dm_slave_wdata/we through internal logic → we_flag_*
required_evidence_slots: Source of we_flag signals in riscv_peripherals.sv
Any multiplexer that selects debug source
Evidence that a debug write can toggle we_flag despite locked state
obligations.uncertainty: we_flag routing may be internal to other modules; need to trace connectivity.

obligations.reason: Reset control registers (via rst_wrapper) may be writable through the debug interface without locking, allowing an attacker to reset security-critical blocks.
obligations.subject: debug JTAG or test interface
obligations.expected_guard: reset control accesses should be gated by reglk_ctrl and/or jtag_unlock
obligations.candidate_path: bus transaction through rst_wrapper from central bus; debug may inject transactions bypassing lock checks
required_evidence_slots: Point where debug transaction enters rst_wrapper
Lock/privilege check logic in rst_wrapper
Test showing debug write to reset registers when lock is set
obligations.uncertainty: Need to confirm that rst_wrapper always checks lock before accepting writes from any master.

obligations.reason: The access control matrix (acc_ctrl) determines privilege-to-resource mapping. If an unauthorized agent (debug, test, low privilege) can modify acc_ctrl, the entire access-control scheme collapses.
obligations.subject: debug interface, test mode, or unprivileged bus master
obligations.expected_guard: acc_ctrl writes must be allowed only for highest privilege or authenticated debug with explicit lock release
obligations.candidate_path: bus write to acc_ctrl address range → acc_ctrl update
state_conditions_to_check: we_flag or lock state controlling acc_ctrl
required_evidence_slots: Address decoder targeting acc_ctrl registers
Write-enable logic for acc_ctrl
Absence of privilege/lock check before updating acc_ctrl
obligations.uncertainty: Exact register map for acc_ctrl is not shown; need to confirm there is no direct bus access without guard.

obligations.reason: The JTAG unlock signal (jtag_unlock) gates powerful debug capabilities. Verify that jtag_unlock cannot be asserted without the correct hash challenge, and that it does not have an unsafe reset default.
obligations.subject: JTAG interface performing challenge/response
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock must be asserted only after a valid hash match (jtag_hash); it must be reset to 0 and not be statically overridden
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG commands → hash computation → jtag_unlock flip-flop
state_conditions_to_check: any direct path from testmode_i or debug_mode_i that forces jtag_unlock
required_evidence_slots: RTL for jtag_unlock generation
Hash comparator implementation
Proof or simulation that jtag_unlock remains low until correct challenge is completed
obligations.uncertainty: dmi_jtag implementation may be in a different repository; jtag_hash width is 256 bits, possibly HMAC.

obligations.reason: The testmode_i input may globally disable access controls, exposing protected assets. Confirm that testmode_i does not bypass lock, privilege, or authentication gates on any protected resource.
obligations.subject: testmode_i assertion (from external test controller)
obligations.operation: reading/writing protected registers, fuses, or bootrom
obligations.object: any security-sensitive resource (fuse, bootrom, peripherals, acc_ctrl, reglk_ctrl, debug lock)
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i must not be able to override security protections; it should be either disabled in hardware or restricted to non-sensitive operations
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i appears as input to riscv_peripherals; check all combinatorial or sequential usage that gates access or forces enable signals
state_conditions_to_check: any AND/OR gate involving testmode_i and lock/privilege signals
required_evidence_slots: All places in RTL where testmode_i influences security controls
Waveform proving that testmode_i can force we_flag or rom_req without authorization
obligations.uncertainty: testmode_i may be safely tied to 0 in the actual instantiation; need to confirm its usage across all instantiated modules.
